payload.text:
Create a page for Give new life to STEM

STEM Goal
“We support schools in clearing obsolete STEM equipment through proper reuse or disposal, while providing corresponding STEM programme support to help departments refresh their ALP and CCA activities.”

Trade-In / Recycle Event
Organise a “STEM Swap & Give Back Week” where schools can bring old robots and related STEM toy components.
Possible intake categories:
•	Working Condition: Fully functional for reuse
•	Repairable: For refurbishment workshops / student engineering projects
•	E-Waste: Non-functional items with battery/electronics for proper recycling

Valuation & Credit Scheme
Offer schools a STEM support services points for returned items that they can use within the STEM ecosystem (e.g., toward newer kits or service credits):
•	Working kit (complete): 10–20 points
•	Functional motor/sensor parts: 2 points
•	Batteries/electronic units for recycling: 1–2 points
•	Cosmetic/plastic-only (non-electronic): 0 point(but recyclable)
•	Lego: 10points per KG

Schools can redeem STEM points for:
•	Procure new STEM kits (Link to EP-Tec Store)
•	ALP or CCA enrichment workshops
•	Refurbished kits for lower-stakes activities
•	Teacher training hours
•	Event/Competition support
•	Lease STEM Kit sets (Link to our leasing site)

**Conceptualizing STEM Application**

I'm now focusing on a web application idea: "Give New Life to STEM." The core is a recycling/trade-in program for schools' old STEM equipment, like robots and sensors, offering "STEM Points" in return. These points are redeemable for services or new gear. I'm starting with a "Help" section as the core focus.


**Refining Program Mechanics**

I've been solidifying the operational details. I've broken down equipment into categories: Working Condition (Reuse), Repairable (Refurbishment), and E-Waste (Recycling), along with associated "STEM Point" values for each. I'm aiming for flexibility, including points for functional parts and even Lego bricks! Now, the emphasis is on features like a point calculator, dashboard for schools, and a Swap Week event. I am very excited about AI integration to improve functionality!
